HVN-006.name: Diao Chan, Idyll Corsage
HVN-006.effect:
Diao Chan Lineage

On Enter: Choose any amount of non-champion objects and put a wither counter on each of them.

Whenever a non-token object an opponent controls is destroyed, you may banish it. If you do, that opponent summons a Flowerbud token.

rule.description: The destroyed object must be banished by Diao Chan's trigger from the graveyard to have its controller summon a Flowerbud token. A Flowerbud token will not be summoned on the destruction of a regalia, which never exists in the graveyard. In addition, if the destroyed card was banished before Diao Chan's trigger resolves, because this trigger's effect did not do the banishing, a Flowerbud token will also not be summoned.
Returning from a combat phase to main phase does not start a new main phase but resumes the previous one. The wither trigger does not happen when returning to main phase from combat.

The main phase trigger for wither counters is one collective trigger. In one resolution, the turn player decides to pay or not pay for objects with wither counters on them, sacrifices all objects where the all wither counters were not paid for, and then remove all wither counters from all objects they control.

rule.description: To generate means to add cards from outside of the game. Those cards must be legitimate Grand Archive TCG cards, and cannot be represented by anything else. A player will fail to generate if they cannot find the cards to add to the game in a reasonable amount of time
Generated cards are removed from each game zone (including the main deck) after each game, before side-boarding.

Tip: Using an altered card can help you differentiate between generated copies and copies that are in your decklist! Generated card alterations must still follow our guidelines that apply to any other Grand Archive TCG card if they would be used in a competitive event.

rule.description: The face down banished by Quicksilver Grail has no properties. It has no name, types, effects, or stats. This means that effects that interact with cards in banishment that require seeing a certain property such as 'Sword' subtype won't be able to interact with this face down card.
The owner of the face down card may look at it at any time.

When Quicksilver Grail is banished to play the face down banished card, you play that card using its default play type. If the card has a reserve cost, that card is activated. If the card has a memory cost, that card is materialized.
The face down banished card being played still checks for elemental requirements and its costs must be paid.

rule.description: Returning from a combat phase to main phase does not start a new main phase but resumes the previous one. The wither trigger does not happen when returning to main phase from combat.

The main phase trigger for wither counters is one collective trigger. In one resolution, the turn player decides to pay or not pay for objects with wither counters on them, sacrifices all objects where the all wither counters were not paid for, and then remove all wither counters from all objects they control.

rule.description: Blastshot Pump's replacement effect causes the attacker to deal combat damage to an additional unit. Because this damage is also considered combat damage, it can trigger On Hit effects accordingly.

rule.description: The main phase trigger for wither counters is one collective trigger. In one resolution, the turn player decides to pay or not pay for objects with wither counters on them, sacrifices all objects where the all wither counters were not paid for, and then remove all wither counters from all objects they control.
Returning from a combat phase to main phase does not start a new main phase but resumes the previous one. The wither trigger does not happen when returning to main phase from combat.

rule.description: The first ability only triggers for the first instance of a Chessman entering each turn and will consider this retroactively. If a Chessmen ally already entered before Field of Rank and Files enters, it can no longer trigger in the same turn.

The second ability only triggers for the first instance of a Chessmen Command card activation and will consider this retroactively in the same manner as the first ability. It will not apply to a second Chessmen Command card if the first did not enter the intent.

rule.description: The first time a Chessman ally enters the field under your control during each of your turns, that ally gets +2 power until end of turn. -> Whenever one or more Chessman allies enter the field under your control for the first time during each of your turns, choose one of them and that ally gets +2 power until end of turn.

rule.description: Siegeable domains are not considered units. This means that being destroyed is not considered a death, and on kill and on death abilities won't trigger. Effects that target units cannot target Siegeable domains without additional typing.
In the Standard format, players skip their recollection phase on their first turn of the game. This means that Xuchang won't trigger.
On hit abilities still trigger when dealing combat damage to a Siegeable domain.
As of the release of HVN1E, the cleave keyword has been changed to attack all attackable objects rather than only units. This includes Siegeable domains.
As of the release of HVN1E, the taunt keyword has been changed to block attack targeting from all attackable objects without taunt rather than only units. This includes Siegeable domains.

rule.description: Returning from a combat phase to main phase does not start a new main phase but resumes the previous one. The wither trigger does not happen when returning to main phase from combat.

Whenever a non-token object an opponent controls is destroyed, you may banish it. If you do, that opponent summons a Flowerbud token.

rule.description: The destroyed object must be banished by Diao Chan's trigger from the graveyard to have its controller summon a Flowerbud token. A Flowerbud token will not be summoned on the destruction of a regalia, which never exists in the graveyard. In addition, if the destroyed card was banished before Diao Chan's trigger resolves, because this trigger's effect did not do the banishing, a Flowerbud token will also not be summoned.
Returning from a combat phase to main phase does not start a new main phase but resumes the previous one. The wither trigger does not happen when returning to main phase from combat.

The main phase trigger for wither counters is one collective trigger. In one resolution, the turn player decides to pay or not pay for objects with wither counters on them, sacrifices all objects where the all wither counters were not paid for, and then remove all wither counters from all objects they control.

rule.description: Blastshot Pump's replacement effect causes the attacker to deal combat damage to an additional unit. Because this damage is also considered combat damage, it can trigger On Hit effects accordingly.

rule.description: Returning from a combat phase to main phase does not start a new main phase but resumes the previous one. The wither trigger does not happen when returning to main phase from combat.

The main phase trigger for wither counters is one collective trigger. HVN-146.name: Xuchang, Frozen Citadel
HVN-146.effect:
(Siegeable — This domain can be attacked. It takes damage in the form of removing durability counters.)

At the beginning of each opponent's recollection phase, that player may banish a card with floating memory from their graveyard. If they don't, the next card they activate this turn costs 2 more to activate.

rule.description: Siegeable domains are not considered units. This means that being destroyed is not considered a death, and on kill and on death abilities won't trigger. Effects that target units cannot target Siegeable domains without additional typing.
In the Standard format, players skip their recollection phase on their first turn of the game. This means that Xuchang won't trigger.
On hit abilities still trigger when dealing combat damage to a Siegeable domain.
As of the release of HVN1E, the cleave keyword has been changed to attack all attackable objects rather than only units. This includes Siegeable domains.
As of the release of HVN1E, the taunt keyword has been changed to block attack targeting from all attackable objects without taunt rather than only units. This includes Siegeable domains.

rule.description: Returning from a combat phase to main phase does not start a new main phase but resumes the previous one. The wither trigger does not happen when returning to main phase from combat.

The main phase trigger for wither counters is one collective trigger. In one resolution, the turn player decides to pay or not pay for objects with wither counters on them, sacrifices all objects where the all wither counters were not paid for, and then remove all wither counters from all objects they control.

rule.description: Seize Fate does not prevent your champion from dying due to having damage counters equal to or above its life stat.

State-based checks that occur to check wher the champion is banished is part of the replacement effect. E.g.
